field_decisions.value: Germany generally requires B2 German for nursing work, while nursing trainees from abroad may need B1 or B2 depending on the federal state.
field_decisions.reason: This is the narrowest defensible route-threshold summary for the product context.
Both routes are directly evidenced by the exam bodies.

field_decisions.value: The Federal Employment Agency reported 35,000 average nursing vacancies in 2023, 270,000 foreign-national qualified nurses (16%) in 2023, and an 18% foreign-worker share in nursing professions in 2025.
field_decisions.reason: These are the cleanest dated official market and workforce figures found in the rerun.

field_decisions.value: BAMF occupational German support includes certificate courses and specialist-language-exam preparation for recognition, including healthcare use cases.
field_decisions.reason: This is a real public ecosystem route, but not proof of product-specific eligibility.

field_decisions.value: Official German nursing recruitment routes include Triple Win for India (Kerala and Telangana), Indonesia, the Philippines and Tunisia, with German-language and specialist nursing-course support.
field_decisions.reason: This is directly stated on the official nursing route page and is relevant context for origin-country targeting.

value: No public source shows that an 8-12 week sprint is enough for most nurses to reach the required proof route.
The 300K+ / 17.8% foreign-nurse claim is contradicted by current BA figures.
Product-specific eligibility under BAMF or BA-linked routes is unverified.
The India-Morocco-Brazil recruiting-route claim is contradicted by current official programme pages.
No defensible market-gap or family-income conversion proof survived the rerun.
field_decisions.reason: These are the main fail-closed reasons the product stays in review.

field_decisions.value: International nurses and employer or recruitment partners preparing candidates for German nursing entry, training, or recognition routes.
field_decisions.omission_reason: Useful positioning context, but still a synthesis rather than a product-safe fact.
No public evidence proves that an 8-12 week sprint is enough for most learners to hit the required route threshold.
Public BAMF and recruitment routes exist, but the rerun found no proof that B1 itself is part of or eligible under those programmes.
The India-Morocco-Brazil framing is contradicted and cannot be used as safe country-scope copy.
The 300K+ / 17.8% figure did not survive current official verification.
No exhaustive market scan was completed, so the absence-of-competition story remains unproven.
No external evidence was found for the family-income conversion framing.
